DARM-ANN
DISTRIBUTED AGENTIC RECURSIVE MEMORY NETWORK
POC v1.0 // Web3 Control Plane // Security-First Build
SYSTEM INITIALIZATION
[ ] Generating cryptographic identity (ECDSA P-256)…
[ ] Opening IndexedDB storage…
[ ] Building LSH index (d=128, 3 tables, ~99.9% recall)…
[ ] Initializing branch DAG ledger…
[ ] Creating genesis block — global chain…
[ ] Spawning 4-layer agent topology…
[ ] Starting EDR monitor + audit log…
[ ] Starting P2P peer fabric (WebRTC/BroadcastChannel)…
[ ] Loading auth chain + identity registry…
Resource budget: <5% CPU idle · <50MB RAM · requestIdleCallback scheduling
All data stays in your browser. Open another tab to form a local mesh.

// TASK ROUTER — SLM TIER CLASSIFIER
LSH-based routing · TinyLM / SLM-Small / SLM-Medium / SLM-Large
SUBMIT TASK
ROUTING RULES
● TinyLM — ping, status, hello, count, check, yes/no (≤10 words)
● SLM-Small — summarize, explain, classify, define, find
● SLM-Medium — analyze, compare, reason, evaluate, design, tradeoff
● SLM-Large — complex synthesis, multi-step, research, proof, architecture
Confidence gate: <0.6 → escalate to next tier
ROUTING HISTORY
// LoRA ADAPTER REGISTRY
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning — versioned on chain
0
Adapters
—
Param Reduction
vs full fine-tune
75%
Memory Reduction
QLoRA NF4
MATHEMATICAL PARAMETERS
Rank r = 16  |  Hidden d = 4096  |  Scale α = 32
Param reduction: 2r/d = —  |  FedAvg speedup: —×
QLoRA memory: 0.5p + 12dr bytes  |  7B model → ~3.5 GB
Convergence rounds: ~—× faster than full FedAvg
ADAPTER CHAIN LOG
// IDENTITY & AUTH — ZERO-KNOWLEDGE LOGIN
Client-side key derivation · public key on-chain · private key never leaves your device
● NOT LOGGED IN
SECURITY ARCHITECTURE
✓ Private key — derived locally from password+salt via PBKDF2 (310,000 iters). Never stored, never transmitted.
✓ Password — used only for key derivation, zeroed from memory immediately after. Never stored anywhere.
✓ Handle — stored on-chain as SHA-256 hash only. Plaintext never written to any ledger.
⚡ On-chain — only: handle-hash, public key, timestamp, capability flags. Zero PII.
⚡ Auth flow — sign a fresh nonce with private key → peers verify against on-chain public key. No password transmitted.
LOGIN — CHALLENGE-RESPONSE
HANDLE
PASSWORD
RECOVERY KEY (optional — provides second factor)
ACTIVE SESSION
REGISTER NEW IDENTITY
HANDLE *
PASSWORD *
CONFIRM PASSWORD *
ACCOUNT TYPE
RECOVERY KEY (optional — acts as second factor salt)
WHAT IS NEVER STORED
✗ Your password — used only for key derivation, then immediately discarded from memory
✗ Your private key — re-derived from password+salt on each login, never persisted
✗ Your plaintext handle — only a SHA-256 hash is written to the auth chain
✗ Your recovery key — used as additional salt only, never stored in any form
✓ What IS stored on auth chain: handle-hash, public key, salt (non-secret), account type, timestamp
REGISTERED AGENT IDENTITIES
No agent accounts registered yet.
PROVISION AGENT ACCOUNT
Agent accounts are provisioned by a logged-in human operator. The agent's keypair is derived deterministically from the agent ID + operator signature.
